reasoning: No specific vaccine for Gonorrhea has been created or approved, and leading candidates are only in Phase II clinical trials (expected to take years for potential approval). England began off-label use of the 4CMenB (meningitis B) vaccine for high-risk individuals in August 2025 due to cross-protection. However, as of late October 2025, the Wikipedia "List of infectious diseases" page still maintained the value "No" for a gonorrhea vaccine. Given the absence of a true targeted vaccine, an upcoming change to "Yes" within the short term depends primarily on an editor classifying the off-label 4CMenB rollout as a "Yes," which has not happened in the three months since the rollout, making such an update unlikely to occur and persist. I am assigning minimum allowed probability (0.05) for the near-term date, and slightly higher probabilities for the later dates to reflect increasing uncertainty.
